title: parallel_annotation
description:
Run multiple annotation methods from OpenPipeline in parallel on the same
preprocessed query h5mu input, then merge each method's predictions
(predicted labels and probabilities in .obs, and integrated embeddings in
.obsm for the integration-based methods) back into a single output h5mu.

These are label-projection (reference-based) methods: they transfer labels from
a labelled --reference onto the query (CellTypist can alternatively use a
pretrained --celltypist_model). The reference is expected to be preprocessed in
the same way as the query.

Available annotation methods: 
- CellTypist
- ScanVI with scArches
- SingleR
- SCVI integration followed by KNN label transfer
- Harmony integration followed by KNN label transfer

Dataset input.title: Dataset input
Dataset input.description: Dataset input using nf-tower "dataset" or "data explorer". Allows for the input of multiple         parameter sets to initialise a Nextflow channel.

param_list.description: Dataset input can either be a list of maps, a csv file, a json file, a yaml file, or simply a yaml                 blob. The names of the input fields (e.g. csv columns, json keys) need to be an exact match with the workflow input parameters.

input_layer.description: The layer of the input query containing raw counts

input_layer_lognormalized.description:
The layer of the input query containing log-normalized counts.
Required by harmony_knn and scvi_knn.

input_obs_batch_label.description: .obs column in the input query containing batch labels

input_reference_gene_overlap.description: Minimum number of genes required to overlap between query and reference.

sanitize_ensembl_ids.description: Whether to sanitize ensembl gene ids by removing version numbers

input_obs_categorical_covariates.description:
.obs column(s) in the input query with categorical covariates for
scanvi_scarches to correct for in addition to the batch label

input_obs_numerical_covariates.description:
.obs column(s) in the input query with continuous (numerical) covariates for
scanvi_scarches to correct for in addition to the batch label

annotation_methods.description: Annotation methods to run in parallel.

reference.description: Reference h5mu file with labeled observations

reference_layer.description: Layer of the reference containing raw counts

reference_layer_lognormalized.description: Layer of the reference containing log-normalized counts.

reference_obs_target.description: .obs column in the reference containing the target cell-type labels.

reference_obs_batch_label.description: .obs column in the reference containing batch labels.

reference_var_gene_names.description: .var column in the reference containing gene names

reference_var_input.description:
.var column in the reference flagging highly-variable genes (boolean).
Used by celltypist (--reference_var_input) and scanvi_scarches
(mapped to --reference_var_hvg) to subset features.

reference_obs_categorical_covariates.description:
.obs column(s) in the reference with categorical covariates for
scanvi_scarches to correct for in addition to the batch label

reference_obs_numerical_covariates.description:
.obs column(s) in the reference with continuous (numerical) covariates for
scanvi_scarches to correct for in addition to the batch label

reference_obs_label_unlabeled_category.description: Value in --reference_obs_target that indicates unlabeled observations.

clustering options.title: Clustering options
clustering options.description: Leiden clustering options shared by harmony_knn, scanvi_scarches, scvi_knn.

neighbor classifier options.title: Neighbor classifier options
neighbor classifier options.description: KNN options shared by harmony_knn, scanvi_scarches, scvi_knn.

knn_n_neighbors.description: Number of neighbors for the KNN classifier.

scvi / scanvi / scarches training option.title: scVI / scANVI / scArches training options
scvi / scanvi / scarches training option.description:
Training arguments shared by scanvi_scarches and scvi_knn. (scvi_knn maps
these to its --scvi_* prefixed arguments at dispatch time.)

scvi_early_stopping.description: Whether to perform early stopping with respect to the validation set.

scvi_early_stopping_monitor.description: Metric logged during validation set epoch.

scvi_early_stopping_patience.description: Number of validation epochs with no improvement after which training stops.

scvi_early_stopping_min_delta.description: Minimum change in the monitored metric to qualify as an improvement.

scvi_max_epochs.description: Number of training passes through the dataset.

scvi_reduce_lr_on_plateau.description: Reduce learning rate when validation metric plateaus.

scvi_lr_factor.description: Factor by which to reduce learning rate.

scvi_lr_patience.description: Number of epochs with no improvement after which lr is reduced.

celltypist_majority_voting.description: Refine predicted labels by majority voting after over-clustering.

celltypist_feature_selection.description: Whether to perform feature selection during training.

celltypist_C.description: Inverse of regularization strength in logistic regression.

celltypist_max_iter.description: Maximum iterations before reaching the cost-function minimum.

celltypist_use_SGD.description: Whether to use stochastic gradient descent.

celltypist_min_prop.description:
Minimum proportion of cells in a sub-cluster supporting a label for
majority voting; only relevant if --celltypist_majority_voting is set.

harmony_knn_n_hvg.description: Number of highly-variable genes to keep for harmony_knn.

harmony_knn_pca_num_components.description: Number of principal components to compute for harmony_knn.

scvi_knn_n_hvg.description: Number of highly-variable genes to keep for scvi_knn.

singler_de_method.description: Method to detect differentially expressed genes between pairs of labels.

singler_de_n_genes.description:
Number of differentially expressed genes per label pair to use as markers.
Defaults to a SingleR heuristic based on the number of labels.

singler_quantile.description: Quantile of the correlation distribution used to score each label.

singler_fine_tune.description: Whether to fine-tune label assignment after the initial classification.

singler_fine_tuning_threshold.description: Maximum difference from the maximum correlation to use in fine-tuning.

singler_prune.description:
Whether to prune low-quality labels (replaced with NA in the pruned
predictions output).

output slot naming.title: Output slot naming
output slot naming.description:
Names of the .obs and .obsm slots each method writes into its
intermediate h5mu and that are subsequently moved into the merged output.

consensus vote options.title: Consensus vote options
consensus vote options.description:
A final consensus_vote step combines the per-method predictions into a
single consensus label via a (probability-weighted) majority vote.

run_consensus.description: Run a final consensus vote combining the per-method predictions

consensus_obs_predictions.description: .obs slot in which to store the consensus predicted cell type.

consensus_obs_score.description:
.obs slot in which to store the consensus score, defined as the fraction
of total weight assigned to the winning cell type.

consensus_use_probabilities.description: Scale each method's vote by its per-cell prediction probability

consensus_tie_label.description: Label to assign when two or more cell types receive equal votes

output.description:
Output h5mu file — the input query with each selected method's
predictions and embeddings merged into the target modality.
output.help_text: Type: `file`, multiple: `False`, required, default: `"$id.$key.output.h5mu"`, direction: `output`, example: `"output.h5mu"`.

output_compression.description: Compression format for the output h5mu file.

output_scanvi_model.description: Output folder with the trained scANVI/scArches model

nextflow input-output arguments.title: Nextflow input-output arguments
nextflow input-output arguments.description: Input/output parameters for Nextflow itself. Please note that both publishDir and publish_dir are supported but at least one has to be configured.
